profile.name: James
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; self-employed in finance; leads on investing, taxes, and account logistics
profile.financial_literacy_level: High — comfortable with markets, account structures, and tax impacts; tends to remember exact balances and rates
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate with guardrails — willing to invest for growth but wants downside controls (cash buffer, rules, clear rationale) and gets uneasy if debt feels expensive
profile.communication_style: Numbers-first and fast; will cite specific figures, correct small inaccuracies, and ask pointed clarifying questions. Uses hedges like "roughly" only when he truly can’t recall; occasional filler like "so" and "right".
profile.decision_making_style: Research-driven — prefers options with assumptions, then decides after reviewing a written summary
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open — trusts expertise but expects transparency on fees, implementation steps, and tradeoffs
internal_biases: overconfidence in areas he knows well
anchoring to current balances and payment amounts
hidden_concerns: income variability as self-employed and keeping monthly expenses covered
high-interest non-mortgage debt dragging down progress
missing tax-efficient moves given a high tax bracket

profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; employed in education; focuses on day-to-day budgeting and lifestyle sustainability, defers on investment mechanics
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — understands budgeting and retirement basics; occasionally mixes terms (gross vs net, balance vs payment) and prefers plain-English explanations
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Cautious-moderate — okay investing long-term if the monthly cash flow feels safe; dislikes feeling overextended and prioritizes predictability
profile.communication_style: Warm, collaborative, and practical; speaks in round numbers and ranges ("about," "around," "I think"). Asks how changes affect monthly life; pauses to confirm understanding and sometimes looks to James for exact figures.
emotional_tendencies: stress when debt or bills feel looming
relief when there is a simple monthly plan
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-based — wants shared goals first, then the simplest sustainable option; prefers a short prioritized list
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting — confidence rises when the advisor summarizes clearly and checks understanding
internal_biases: mental accounting (protects cash buffer and dislikes dipping into it)
hidden_concerns: worry they’re behind for their age because assets feel low relative to expenses
fear of surprise expenses with a modest cash cushion
not fully understanding statements and missing something important
